title: Add a suggested work-order condition
definition: The native mutation flow initiated from an available suggested-row add control to attach that suggested filter as an existing item condition.

facts.statement: The add flow is offered only when the native already-present test does not find an existing condition with matching scalar and string filter identity.
The flow operates on the generated suggestion represented by its row rather than recomputing a threshold from the work-order quantity.
Activating the available control added a second, distinct complete filter; both filters remained exact after closing and reopening the editor, and cleanup restored the original single filter.

uncertainties.statement: The pinned sequence covers activation, exact identity, editor close/reopen persistence, and cleanup, but not a disk save/reload cycle or a controlled initial cached-satisfaction transition.

positive: Activating an available add control creates an existing item condition with the generated suggestion's copied filter and fixed comparison.
boundary: A matching existing filter suppresses entry to the flow even if operator, comparison value, or contained-reagent indexes differ.
counterexamples: A nonmatching scalar or string filter must leave the add flow available; the resulting threshold must not come from order quantity.

absenceGuards: Do not enter the add flow when the native already-present test suppresses its control.
Do not claim mutation success or persistence without a post-action state slice.

stateSlice: existing condition ids before and after
requiredFrames: Reopened editor after save persistence check
